seed_problem: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: replay the online spot-vs-on-demand deadline problem as a static full-trace optimization (input) AND add a per-run startup cost plus variable spot throughput/availability (output constraint), turning it into a multiple-choice covering knapsack with run-segmentation whose optimum is NP-hard and where greedy/prefix/local-search strategies diverge.
title: Spot-Flour Milling: Offline Replay of the Bakery Power Trace
strategies: trivial baseline: run On-Demand for the first ceil(W/q) slots as one contiguous block (matches the checker baseline)
prefix greedy: fill from slot 1 with the cheaper-per-sack available mode each slot until demand is met (one run, cheap spot where possible)
cost-per-sack knapsack: select the globally cheapest per-sack slot-options to cover W, then repair the startup penalty by clustering
local search: hill-climb the full objective (operating cost + K*runs) from a feasible start, swapping modes and merging runs
